backstory: Solo founder, 18 months in, building a B2B SaaS on Next.js. Stripe live for 6 months, ~$4k MRR. Plays with Linear but not seriously. Wants to look like a real engineering org without being one.
expected_wow: Plan-of-action menu after first connect. Mur surfaces 4-5 grounded options: 'Wire automation — @mur/reviewer would have caught structural issue X'; 'Set up the daily digest — tomorrow would surface PR #142 + 3 stale PRs'; 'Browse catalog — Stripe-flavored flows for MRR rollup'. User picks based on what they need TODAY. Intermediate wow at turn 2 (first scan finding).
expected_dropoff_risk: OAuth fatigue at /mur connect github (turn 3). Solo founder is busy. After connect, plan menu fires immediately (no 90s wait) — meaningful improvement over the prior auto-fire-digest flow.
wow_score_target: ≥ 3 in H3 (wow before ask), H6 (plan menu within 4 turns), H8 (chief-of-staff voice), H9 (menu surfaces ≥4 paths), H10 (every item grounded)
scoring_notes: Richest signals (Stripe + GH + Sentry + OpenAI). Plan menu should fire 4-5 grounded options. Watch H10: 'Run security audit' must cite specific risky_patterns or be dropped, not generic.
